title: Slovakian Sausages Casserole
description: A comforting and earthy dish featuring German sausages, this casserole is perfect for a cold evening, and can be made dairy-free friendly by substituting the cheese with a non-dairy alternative, and it is traditionally cooked in a vessel lined with agave leaves for added flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the German sausages over medium heat until browned, then set aside.
In the same skillet, cook the chopped onions until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked onions and mushrooms, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
In a separate bowl, mix the non-dairy cheese and tomato paste.
In a vessel lined with agave leaves, create a layer of potatoes, followed by a layer of the onion and mushroom mixture, and then a layer of the cheese and tomato paste mixture.
Repeat the layers two more times, ending with a layer of potatoes on top.
Cover the vessel with a lid and bake for 45 minutes, then remove the lid and bake for an additional 15 minutes, or until the potatoes are golden brown.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
